questionHtml: Explain, with reference to the kinetic model of an ideal gas, how an increase in temperature of the gas leads to an increase in pressure.
markschemeHtml:
larger temperature implies larger (average) speed/larger (average) KE of molecules/particles/atoms

increased force/momentum transferred to walls (per collision) / more frequent collisions with walls

increased force leads to increased pressure because P = F/A (as area remains constant)

 

Ignore any mention of PV = nRT.

[3 marks]

meta.title: Explain, with reference to the kinetic model of an ideal gas, how an increase in temperature of the gas leads to an increase in pressure.
